message.content:
GUIDELINES
Capturing AI Logs
Every 8x contest requires you to submit the AI conversation logs from your build. They live inside your repository at /ai-logs/ — we check the folder when you submit.

Files in /ai-logs/: .txt .md .jsonl — at least one file required, no upload size limit.

Required Log Structure
Each interaction in your logs must clearly distinguish the prompt from the AI's response. The simplest format is Markdown headers:

## Prompt
What prompt you typed to the AI — as specific as possible.

## Response
The full response the AI gave you.

---

## Prompt
Your next prompt...

## Response
The next response...
You can use any separator style as long as prompts and responses are clearly labeled. Raw exported files from Claude Code or Cursor are accepted as-is.

Tool-Specific Instructions
Claude Code
Auto-captured
Claude Code automatically saves conversation logs as Markdown files under ~/.claude/projects/. No extra steps needed — just find and upload them.

# List your Claude Code project logs
ls ~/.claude/projects/

# Copy logs into the /ai-logs/ folder of your repo
mkdir -p ./ai-logs
cp ~/.claude/projects/<your-project>/*.md ./ai-logs/

# Commit and push
git add ai-logs && git commit -m "add ai logs" && git push
Claude Code documentation
Cursor
Export your chat history from the Cursor command palette.

1. Open command palette: Cmd+Shift+P / Ctrl+Shift+P

2. Search: Export Chat History

3. Save the export into ./ai-logs/ as .txt, then commit and push

Cursor documentation
Windsurf / Codeium
Windsurf does not have a built-in export — copy your conversation manually.

1. Open the Windsurf chat panel

2. Select all conversation text (Ctrl+A)

3. Paste into ./ai-logs/<thread>.txt, format with Prompt / Response headers, then commit and push

Windsurf documentation
Any other AI tool
Generic
Use this shell script to record a terminal session with timestamps. Run it before you start working and it will capture everything automatically.

#!/bin/bash
# ai-log-capture.sh
# Records your terminal session to a file.
# Usage: bash ai-log-capture.sh my-session.txt
# Press Ctrl+D or type 'exit' to stop recording.

OUTPUT="${1:-ai-session.txt}"
echo "Recording session to $OUTPUT — press Ctrl+D to stop."
script -q "$OUTPUT"
echo "Session saved to $OUTPUT"
To format a raw paste into clean Prompt / Response Markdown, run this Python helper:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# format-ai-log.py
# Usage: python format-ai-log.py raw.txt > formatted.md

import sys, re

def format_log(text):
    # Split on common AI tool separators
    blocks = re.split(r'\n(?=You:|Human:|User:|Assistant:|AI:)', text)
    out = []
    for block in blocks:
        if re.match(r'^(You:|Human:|User:)', block):
            content = re.sub(r'^(You:|Human:|User:)\s*', '', block).strip()
            out.append(f"## Prompt\n{content}")
        elif re.match(r'^(Assistant:|AI:)', block):
            content = re.sub(r'^(Assistant:|AI:)\s*', '', block).strip()
            out.append(f"## Response\n{content}")
    return "\n\n---\n\n".join(out)

with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
    print(format_log(f.read()))
Tips for High-Quality Logs
Include your full conversation — reviewers look for how you iterated, not just the final prompt.
Do not edit or clean up AI responses. Commit raw exports whenever possible.
If you used multiple tools, drop a separate file for each into /ai-logs/ — no need to zip.
Your repo is public, so your logs are public. Scrub any API keys or secrets before committing.
Minimum: at least 1 file in /ai-logs/. No maximum on conversation length or file count.

BEFORE YOU START
Build a Music Personality App — Spotify-Wrapped Style Analyzer
Generate a shareable personality profile from your Spotify data — music DNA, listening archetypes, and a "what your taste says about you" breakdown.

$50 top prize
Deadline: May 17, 2026
Set up AI logging NOW — before you write a single line of code

Commit your AI conversations to /ai-logs/in your repo as you build. We check the folder when you submit — if it's missing, the submission is rejected. Logs cannot be reconstructed retroactively.

Claude Code

Logs are saved automatically to ~/.claude/projects/<name>/
Copy the .md / .jsonl files into ./ai-logs/ in your repo, then commit and push
Cursor

Open Chat panel → click "..." → Export conversation
Save the export into ./ai-logs/ as .txt, then commit and push
Windsurf / Codeium

Open Cascade → right-click thread → Copy all messages
Paste into ./ai-logs/<thread>.txt, then commit and push
Read full guide →
Your repo must be public.

Submissions require a public github.com URL — ZIP uploads are no longer accepted. If your work is on a private fork, push a sanitized public mirror or add collaborators before submitting.

⭐
Get Priority Review

Did you use our starter template? Please star the repository on GitHub! We actively prioritize grading submissions from developers who support our open-source templates.

WHAT YOU NEED TO SUBMIT
Public GitHub URLPublic github.com repo with an /ai-logs/ folder containing your AI conversations
REQUIRED
Loom walkthroughQuick intro + full app demo — loom.com
REQUIRED
ScreenshotsUp to 15 screens of your finished app
REQUIRED
ReflectionWhat was easy, difficult, what you learned
REQUIRED
Resume / CVYour background — helps judges understand you
OPTIONAL
HOW YOU'LL BE JUDGED
1
Screenshots

First thing judges see — make them polished.

2
Loom walkthrough

Shows the app end-to-end, proves it works.

3
AI logs

Quality of your AI usage and iteration.

4
Code quality

Clean structure, sensible decisions.

5
Reflection

Honest and thoughtful self-assessment.

attachment.content:
- ruflo-core:ruflo-status: Show Ruflo system health, MCP server status, and active agents
- ruflo-core:witness: Manage and verify a cryptographically-signed fix manifest with temporal history (ADR-103)
- ruflo-swarm:swarm: Initialize, monitor, and manage multi-agent swarms
- ruflo-swarm:watch: Live-stream swarm events and agent activity in real time
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot-status: Quick autopilot progress summary with task completion stats
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot: Enable, configure, or disable autonomous task completion
- ruflo-federation:federation: Manage cross-installation agent federation
- claude-mem:mem-search: Search claude-mem's persistent cross-session memory database. Use when user asks "did we already solve this?", "how did we do X last time?", or needs work from previous sessions.
- claude-mem:babysit: Watch a pull request or review cycle until it is ready to merge. Use when asked to babysit, monitor, or keep checking PR comments, reviews, and CI until all actionable issues are resolved.
- claude-mem:timeline-report: Generate a "Journey Into [Project]" narrative report analyzing a project's entire development history from claude-mem's timeline. Use when asked for a timeline report, project history analysis, development journey, or full project report.
- claude-mem:pathfinder: Map a codebase into feature-grouped flowcharts, identify duplicated concerns across features, and propose a unified architecture. Use when asked to "find the ideal path," unify duplicated systems, or audit architecture before a refactor. Emits a proposed unified flowchart plus per-system /make-plan prompts.
- claude-mem:knowledge-agent: Build and query AI-powered knowledge bases from claude-mem observations. Use when users want to create focused "brains" from their observation history, ask questions about past work patterns, or compile expertise on specific topics.
- claude-mem:smart-explore: Token-optimized structural code search using tree-sitter AST parsing. Use instead of reading full files when you need to understand code structure, find functions, or explore a codebase efficiently.
- claude-mem:make-plan: Create a detailed, phased implementation plan with documentation discovery. Use when asked to plan a feature, task, or multi-step implementation — especially before executing with do.
- claude-mem:version-bump: Automated semantic versioning and release workflow for Claude Code plugins. Handles version increments across package.json, marketplace.json, plugin.json manifests, npm publishing (so `npx claude-mem@X.Y.Z` resolves), build verification, git tagging, GitHub releases, and changelog generation.
- claude-mem:how-it-works: Explain how claude-mem captures observations, when memory injection kicks in, and where data lives. Use when the user asks "how does claude-mem work?" or "what is this thing doing?".
- claude-mem:do: Execute a phased implementation plan using subagents. Use when asked to execute, run, or carry out a plan — especially one created by make-plan.
- claude-mem:wowerpoint: Turn one document into a kawaii NotebookLM slide-deck PDF. Use for "wowerpoint this", "make a deck about <file>", "turn this report into slides", or any request to render a single document as shareable narrative slides.
- claude-mem:learn-codebase: Prime a codebase by reading every source file in full. Use when starting work on a new or unfamiliar project, or when the user asks to "learn the codebase", "read the codebase", "prime", or "get up to speed".
- ruflo-core:discover-plugins: Discover and recommend ruflo plugins based on your workflow, installed MCP tools, and current task
- ruflo-core:ruflo-doctor: Run health checks on the Ruflo installation and fix common issues
- ruflo-core:witness: Sign, verify, and track fix-marker regressions over time using a deterministic Ed25519 witness manifest. Works in any project — clone the toolkit, run init, register fixes, regen on each release.
- ruflo-core:init-project: Initialize a new Ruflo project with MCP tools, hooks, and agent configuration
- ruflo-swarm:swarm-init: Initialize a multi-agent swarm with anti-drift configuration
- ruflo-swarm:monitor-stream: Stream live swarm events using the Monitor tool for real-time observability
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot-predict: Use learned patterns and current state to predict the optimal next action
- ruflo-autopilot:autopilot-loop: Run an autonomous /loop iteration -- check progress, work on next task, schedule next wake
- ruflo-federation:federation-audit: Query federation audit logs with compliance filtering
- ruflo-federation:federation-status: Show federation health — peers, sessions, trust levels, and message metrics
- ruflo-federation:federation-init: Initialize federation on this node — generate keypair and configure peers
- ui-ux-pro-max:ui-ux-pro-max: UI/UX design intelligence for web and mobile. Includes 50+ styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 161 product types, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 10 stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and HTML/CSS). Actions: plan, build, create, design, implement, review, fix, improve, optimize, enhance, refactor, and check UI/UX code. Projects: website, landing page, dashboard, admin panel, e-commerce, SaaS, portfolio, blog, and mobile app. Elements: button, modal, navbar, sidebar, card, table, form, and chart. Styles: glassmorphism, claymorphism, minimalism, brutalism, neumorphism, bento grid, dark mode, responsive, skeuomorphism, and flat design. Topics: color systems, accessibility, animation, layout, typography, font pairing, spacing, interaction states, shadow, and gradient. Integrations: shadcn/ui MCP for component search and examples.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Self-Learning Hooks System

Intelligent workflow automation with pattern learning and adaptive routing

Usage: claude-flow hooks <subcommand> [options]

Subcommands:
  - pre-edit        - Get context before editing files
  - post-edit       - Record editing outcomes for learning
  - pre-command     - Assess risk before executing commands
  - post-command    - Record command execution outcomes
  - pre-task        - Record task start and get agent suggestions
  - post-task       - Record task completion for learning
  - session-end     - End current session and persist state
  - session-restore - Restore a previous session
  - route           - Route tasks to optimal agents
  - explain         - Explain routing decisions
  - pretrain        - Bootstrap intelligence from repository
  - build-agents    - Generate optimized agent configs
  - metrics         - View learning metrics dashboard
  - transfer        - Transfer patterns from another project
  - list            - List all registered hooks
  - worker          - Background worker management (12 workers)
  - progress        - Check V3 implementation progress
  - statusline      - Generate dynamic statusline display
  - coverage-route  - Route tasks based on coverage gaps (ruvector)
  - coverage-suggest- Suggest coverage improvements
  - coverage-gaps   - List all coverage gaps with agents
  - token-optimize - Token optimization (agentic-flow integration)
  - model-route    - Route to optimal model (haiku/sonnet/opus)
  - model-outcome  - Record model routing outcome
  - model-stats    - View model routing statistics
  - 
  - Agent Teams:
  - teammate-idle  - Handle idle teammate (auto-assign tasks)
  - task-completed - Handle task completion (train patterns)

Run "claude-flow hooks <subcommand> --help" for subcommand help

V3 Features:
  - 🧠 ReasoningBank adaptive learning
  - ⚡ Flash Attention (2.49x-7.47x speedup)
  - 🔍 AgentDB integration (150x faster search)
  - 📊 84.8% SWE-Bench solve rate
  - 🎯 32.3% token reduction
  - 🚀 2.8-4.4x speed improvement
  - 👥 Agent Teams integration (auto task assignment)

Self-Learning Hooks System

Intelligent workflow automation with pattern learning and adaptive routing

Usage: claude-flow hooks <subcommand> [options]

Subcommands:
  - pre-edit        - Get context before editing files
  - post-edit       - Record editing outcomes for learning
  - pre-command     - Assess risk before executing commands
  - post-command    - Record command execution outcomes
  - pre-task        - Record task start and get agent suggestions
  - post-task       - Record task completion for learning
  - session-end     - End current session and persist state
  - session-restore - Restore a previous session
  - route           - Route tasks to optimal agents
  - explain         - Explain routing decisions
  - pretrain        - Bootstrap intelligence from repository
  - build-agents    - Generate optimized agent configs
  - metrics         - View learning metrics dashboard
  - transfer        - Transfer patterns from another project
  - list            - List all registered hooks
  - worker          - Background worker management (12 workers)
  - progress        - Check V3 implementation progress
  - statusline      - Generate dynamic statusline display
  - coverage-route  - Route tasks based on coverage gaps (ruvector)
  - coverage-suggest- Suggest coverage improvements
  - coverage-gaps   - List all coverage gaps with agents
  - token-optimize - Token optimization (agentic-flow integration)
  - model-route    - Route to optimal model (haiku/sonnet/opus)
  - model-outcome  - Record model routing outcome
  - model-stats    - View model routing statistics
  - 
  - Agent Teams:
  - teammate-idle  - Handle idle teammate (auto-assign tasks)
  - task-completed - Handle task completion (train patterns)

Run "claude-flow hooks <subcommand> --help" for subcommand help

  - ⚡ Flash Attention (2.49x-7.47x speedup)

  - 📊 84.8% SWE-Bench solve rate
  - 🎯 32.3% token reduction
  - 🚀 2.8-4.4x speed improvement
  - 👥 Agent Teams integration (auto task assignment)

attachment.stdout:
Self-Learning Hooks System

Intelligent workflow automation with pattern learning and adaptive routing

Usage: claude-flow hooks <subcommand> [options]

Subcommands:
  - pre-edit        - Get context before editing files
  - post-edit       - Record editing outcomes for learning
  - pre-command     - Assess risk before executing commands
  - post-command    - Record command execution outcomes
  - pre-task        - Record task start and get agent suggestions
  - post-task       - Record task completion for learning
  - session-end     - End current session and persist state
  - session-restore - Restore a previous session
  - route           - Route tasks to optimal agents
  - explain         - Explain routing decisions
  - pretrain        - Bootstrap intelligence from repository
  - build-agents    - Generate optimized agent configs
  - metrics         - View learning metrics dashboard
  - transfer        - Transfer patterns from another project
  - list            - List all registered hooks
  - worker          - Background worker management (12 workers)
  - progress        - Check V3 implementation progress
  - statusline      - Generate dynamic statusline display
  - coverage-route  - Route tasks based on coverage gaps (ruvector)
  - coverage-suggest- Suggest coverage improvements
  - coverage-gaps   - List all coverage gaps with agents
  - token-optimize - Token optimization (agentic-flow integration)
  - model-route    - Route to optimal model (haiku/sonnet/opus)
  - model-outcome  - Record model routing outcome
  - model-stats    - View model routing statistics
  - 
  - Agent Teams:
  - teammate-idle  - Handle idle teammate (auto-assign tasks)
  - task-completed - Handle task completion (train patterns)

Run "claude-flow hooks <subcommand> --help" for subcommand help

V3 Features:
  - 🧠 ReasoningBank adaptive learning
  - ⚡ Flash Attention (2.49x-7.47x speedup)
  - 🔍 AgentDB integration (150x faster search)
  - 📊 84.8% SWE-Bench solve rate
  - 🎯 32.3% token reduction
  - 🚀 2.8-4.4x speed improvement
  - 👥 Agent Teams integration (auto task assignment)

Everything Claude Code 4 5 ![Everything Claude Code — the performance system for AI agent harnesses](assets/hero.png) 6 7 [![Stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/affaan-m/everything-claude-code?style=flat)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/stargazers) 8 [![Forks](https://img.shields.io/github/forks/affaan-m/everything-claude-code?style=flat)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/network/members) 9 [![Contributors](https://img.shields.io/github/contributors/affaan-m/everything-claude-code?style=flat)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/graphs/contributors) 10 [![npm ecc-universal](https://img.shields.io/npm/dw/ecc-universal?label=ecc-universal%20weekly%20downloads&logo=npm)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/ecc-universal) 11 [![npm ecc-agentshield](https://img.shields.io/npm/dw/ecc-agentshield?label=ecc-agentshield%20weekly%20downloads&logo=npm)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/ecc-agentshield) 12 [![GitHub App Install](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub%20App-150%20installs-2ea44f?logo=github)](https://github.com/marketplace/ecc-tools) 13 [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) 14 ![Shell](https://img.shields.io/badge/-Shell-4EAA25?logo=gnu-bash&logoColor=white) 15 ![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/-TypeScript-3178C6?logo=typescript&logoColor=white) 16 ![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/-Python-3776AB?logo=python&logoColor=white) 17 ![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/-Go-00ADD8?logo=go&logoColor=white) 18 ![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/-Java-ED8B00?logo=openjdk&logoColor=white) 19 ![Perl](https://img.shields.io/badge/-Perl-39457E?logo=perl&logoColor=white) 20 ![Markdown](https://img.shields.io/badge/-Markdown-000000?logo=markdown&logoColor=white) 21 22 > **182K+ stars** | **28K+ forks** | **170+ contributors** | **12+ language ecosystems** | **Anthropic Hackathon Winner** 23 24 --- 25 26
27 28 **Language / 语言 / 語言 / Dil / Язык / Ngôn ngữ** 29 30 [**English**](README.md) | [Português (Brasil)](docs/pt-BR/README.md) | [简体中文](README.zh-CN.md) | [繁體中文](docs/zh-TW/README.md) | [日本語](docs/ja-JP/README.md) | [한국어](docs/ko-KR/README.md) 31 | [Türkçe](docs/tr/README.md) | [Русский](docs/ru/README.md) | [Tiếng Việt](docs/vi-VN/README.md) 32 33
34 35 --- 36 37 **The performance optimization system for AI agent harnesses. From an Anthropic hackathon winner.** 38 39 Not just configs. A complete system: skills, instincts, memory optimization, continuous learning, security scanning, and research-first development. Production-ready agents, skills, hooks, rules, MCP configurations, and legacy command shims evolved over 10+ months of intensive daily use building real products. 40 41 Works across **Claude Code**, **Codex**, **Cursor**, **OpenCode**, **Gemini**, **GitHub Copilot**, and other AI agent harnesses. 42 43 ECC v2.0.0-rc.1 adds the public Hermes operator story on top of that reusable layer: start with the [Hermes setup guide](docs/HERMES-SETUP.md), then review the [rc.1 release notes](docs/releases/2.0.0-rc.1/release-notes.md) and [cross-harness architecture](docs/architecture/cross-harness.md). 44 45 46 --- 47 48 49 50 56 62 69 75 76
51 52 ECC Pro
53 Private repos · GitHub App · $19/seat/mo 54 55 		57 58 Sponsor
59 Fund the OSS · From $5/mo 60 61 		63 64 Community 65
66 Discussions · Q&A · Show & Tell 67 68 		70 71 GitHub App
72 Install · PR audits · Free tier 73 74
77 78 **OSS stays free.** This repo is MIT-licensed forever. ECC Pro is the hosted GitHub App for private repos. Sponsors and Pro subscribers fund the work — that's why a single maintainer ships weekly across 7 harnesses. 79 80 --- 81 82 ## The Guides 83 84 This repo is the raw code only. The guides explain everything. 85 86 87 88 93 98 103 104 105 106 107 108 109
89 90 91 92 94 95 96 97 99 100 101 102
Shorthand Guide
Setup, foundations, philosophy. Read this first.		Longform Guide
Token optimization, memory persistence, evals, parallelization.		Security Guide
Attack vectors, sandboxing, sanitization, CVEs, AgentShield.
110 111 | Topic | What You'll Learn | 112 |-------|-------------------| 113 | Token Optimization | Model selection, system prompt slimming, background processes | 114 | Memory Persistence | Hooks that save/load context across sessions automatically | 115 | Continuous Learning | Auto-extract patterns from sessions into reusable skills | 116 | Verification Loops | Checkpoint vs continuous evals, grader types, pass@k metrics | 117 | Parallelization | Git worktrees, cascade method, when to scale instances | 118 | Subagent Orchestration | The context problem, iterative retrieval pattern | 119 120 --- 121 122 ## What's New 123 124 ### v2.0.0-rc.1 — Surface Refresh, Operator Workflows, and ECC 2.0 Alpha (Apr 2026) 125 126 - **Dashboard GUI** — New Tkinter-based desktop application (`ecc_dashboard.py` or `npm run dashboard`) with dark/light theme toggle, font customization, and project logo in header and taskbar. 127 - **Public surface synced to the live repo** — metadata, catalog counts, plugin manifests, and install-facing docs now match the actual OSS surface: 60 agents, 229 skills, and 75 legacy command shims. 128 - **Operator and outbound workflow expansion** — `brand-voice`, `social-graph-ranker`, `connections-optimizer`, `customer-billing-ops`, `ecc-tools-cost-audit`, `google-workspace-ops`, `project-flow-ops`, and `workspace-surface-audit` round out the operator lane. 129 - **Media and launch tooling** — `manim-video`, `remotion-video-creation`, and upgraded social publishing surfaces make technical explainers and launch content part of the same system. 130 - **Framework and product surface growth** — `nestjs-patterns`, richer Codex/OpenCode install surfaces, and expanded cross-harness packaging keep the repo usable beyond Claude Code alone. 131 - **ECC 2.0 alpha is in-tree** — the Rust control-plane prototype in `ecc2/` now builds locally and exposes `dashboard`, `start`, `sessions`, `status`, `stop`, `resume`, and `daemon` commands. It is usable as an alpha, not yet a general release. 132 - **Operator status snapshots** — `ecc status --markdown --write status.md` turns the local state store into a portable handoff covering readiness, active sessions, skill-run health, install health, pending governance events, and linked work items from Linear/GitHub/handoffs. Use `ecc work-items upsert ...` for manual entries, `ecc work-items sync-github --repo owner/repo` for PR/issue queue state, and `ecc status --exit-code` to fail automation when readiness needs attention. 133 - **Ecosystem hardening** — AgentShield, ECC Tools cost controls, billing portal work, and website refreshes continue to ship around the core plugin instead of drifting into separate silos. 134 135 ### v1.9.0 — Selective Install & Language Expansion (Mar 2026) 136 137 - **Selective install architecture** — Manifest-driven install pipeline with `install-plan.js` and `install-apply.js` for targeted component installation. State store tracks what's installed and enables incremental updates. 138 - **6 new agents** — `typescript-reviewer`, `pytorch-build-resolver`, `java-build-resolver`, `java-reviewer`, `kotlin-reviewer`, `kotlin-build-resolver` expand language coverage to 10 languages. 139 - **New skills** — `pytorch-patterns` for deep learning workflows, `documentation-lookup` for API reference research, `bun-runtime` and `nextjs-turbopack` for modern JS toolchains, plus 8 operational domain skills and `mcp-server-patterns`. 140 - **Session & state infrastructure** — SQLite state store with query CLI, session adapters for structured recording, skill evolution foundation for self-improving skills. 141 - **Orchestration overhaul** — Harness audit scoring made deterministic, orchestration status and launcher compatibility hardened, observer loop prevention with 5-layer guard. 142 - **Observer reliability** — Memory explosion fix with throttling and tail sampling, sandbox access fix, lazy-start logic, and re-entrancy guard. 143 - **12 language ecosystems** — New rules for Java, PHP, Perl, Kotlin/Android/KMP, C++, and Rust join existing TypeScript, Python, Go, and common rules. 144 - **Community contributions** — Korean and Chinese translations, biome hook optimization, video processing skills, operational skills, PowerShell installer, Antigravity IDE support. 145 - **CI hardening** — 19 test failure fixes, catalog count enforcement, install manifest validation, and full test suite green. 146 147 ### v1.8.0 — Harness Performance System (Mar 2026) 148 149 - **Harness-first release** — ECC is now explicitly framed as an agent harness performance system, not just a config pack. 150 - **Hook reliability overhaul** — SessionStart root fallback, Stop-phase session summaries, and script-based hooks replacing fragile inline one-liners. 151 - **Hook runtime controls** — `ECC_HOOK_PROFILE=minimal|standard|strict` and `ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS=...` for runtime gating without editing hook files. 152 - **New harness commands** — `/harness-audit`, `/loop-start`, `/loop-status`, `/quality-gate`, `/model-route`. 153 - **NanoClaw v2** — model routing, skill hot-load, session branch/search/export/compact/metrics. 154 - **Cross-harness parity** — behavior tightened across Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, and Codex app/CLI. 155 - **997 internal tests passing** — full suite green after hook/runtime refactor and compatibility updates. 156 157 ### v1.7.0 — Cross-Platform Expansion & Presentation Builder (Feb 2026) 158 159 - **Codex app + CLI support** — Direct `AGENTS.md`-based Codex support, installer targeting, and Codex docs 160 - **`frontend-slides` skill** — Zero-dependency HTML presentation builder with PPTX conversion guidance and strict viewport-fit rules 161 - **5 new generic business/content skills** — `article-writing`, `content-engine`, `market-research`, `investor-materials`, `investor-outreach` 162 - **Broader tool coverage** — Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode support tightened so the same repo ships cleanly across all major harnesses 163 - **992 internal tests** — Expanded validation and regression coverage across plugin, hooks, skills, and packaging 164 165 ### v1.6.0 — Codex CLI, AgentShield & Marketplace (Feb 2026) 166 167 - **Codex CLI support** — New `/codex-setup` command generates `codex.md` for OpenAI Codex CLI compatibility 168 - **7 new skills** — `search-first`, `swift-actor-persistence`, `swift-protocol-di-testing`, `regex-vs-llm-structured-text`, `content-hash-cache-pattern`, `cost-aware-llm-pipeline`, `skill-stocktake` 169 - **AgentShield integration** — `/security-scan` skill runs AgentShield directly from Claude Code; 1282 tests, 102 rules 170 - **GitHub Marketplace** — ECC Tools GitHub App live at [github.com/marketplace/ecc-tools](https://github.com/marketplace/ecc-tools) with free/pro/enterprise tiers 171 - **30+ community PRs merged** — Contributions from 30 contributors across 6 languages 172 - **978 internal tests** — Expanded validation suite across agents, skills, commands, hooks, and rules 173 174 ### v1.4.1 — Bug Fix (Feb 2026) 175 176 - **Fixed instinct import content loss** — `parse_instinct_file()` was silently dropping all content after frontmatter (Action, Evidence, Examples sections) during `/instinct-import`. **Recommended default:** install the Claude Code plugin, then copy only the rule folders you actually want. 213 - **Use the manual installer only if** you want finer-grained control, want to avoid the plugin path entirely, or your Claude Code build has trouble resolving the self-hosted marketplace entry. 214 - **Do not stack install methods.** The most common broken setup is: `/plugin install` first, then `install.sh --profile full` or `npx ecc-install --profile full` afterward. 215 216 If you already layered multiple installs and things look duplicated, skip straight to [Reset / Uninstall ECC](#reset--uninstall-ecc). 217 218 ### Low-context / no-hooks path 219 220 If hooks feel too global or you only want ECC's rules, agents, commands, and core workflow skills, skip the plugin and use the minimal manual profile: 221 222 ```bash 223 ./install.sh --profile minimal --target claude 224 ``` 225 226 ```powershell 227 .\install.ps1 --profile minimal --target claude 228 # or 229 npx ecc-install --profile minimal --target claude 230 ``` 231 232 This profile intentionally excludes `hooks-runtime`. 233 234 If you want the normal core profile but need hooks off, use: 235 236 ```bash 237 ./install.sh --profile core --without baseline:hooks --target claude 238 ``` 239 240 Add hooks later only if you want runtime enforcement: 241 242 ```bash 243 ./install.sh --target claude --modules hooks-runtime 244 ``` 245 246 ### Find the right components first 247 248 If you are not sure which ECC profile or component to install, ask the packaged advisor from any project: 249 250 ```bash 251 npx ecc consult "security reviews" --target claude 252 ``` 253 254 It returns matching components, related profiles, and preview/install commands. Use the preview command before installing if you want to inspect the exact file plan. 255 256 For production ML/MLOps workflows, keep the install opt-in and component-scoped: 257 258 ```bash 259 npx ecc consult "mlops training model deployment" --target claude 260 npx ecc install --profile minimal --target claude --with capability:machine-learning 261 ``` 262 263 ### Step 1: Install the Plugin (Recommended) 264 265 > NOTE: The plugin is convenient, but the OSS installer below is still the most reliable path if your Claude Code build has trouble resolving self-hosted marketplace entries. 266 267 ```bash 268 # Add marketplace 269 /plugin marketplace add https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code 270 271 # Install plugin 272 /plugin install ecc@ecc 273 ``` 274 275 ### Naming + Migration Note 276 277 ECC now has three public identifiers, and they are not interchangeable: 278 279 - GitHub source repo: `affaan-m/everything-claude-code` 280 - Claude marketplace/plugin identifier: `ecc@ecc` 281 - npm package: `ecc-universal` 282 283 This is intentional. Anthropic marketplace/plugin installs are keyed by a canonical plugin identifier, so ECC uses `ecc@ecc` to keep tool names and slash-command namespaces short enough for strict Desktop/API validators. Older posts may still show the former long marketplace identifier; treat that as a legacy alias only. Separately, the npm package stayed on `ecc-universal`, so npm installs and marketplace installs intentionally use different names. 284 285 ### Step 2: Install Rules Only If You Need Them 286 287 > WARNING: **Important:** Claude Code plugins cannot distribute `rules` automatically. 288 > 289 > If you already installed ECC via `/plugin install`, **do not run `./install.sh --profile full`, `.\install.ps1 --profile full`, or `npx ecc-install --profile full` afterward**. The plugin already loads ECC skills, commands, and hooks. Running the full installer after a plugin install copies those same surfaces into your user directories and can create duplicate skills plus duplicate runtime behavior. 290 > 291 > For plugin installs, manually copy only the `rules/` directories you want under `~/.claude/rules/ecc/`. Start with `rules/common` plus one language or framework pack you actually use. Do not copy every rules directory unless you explicitly want all of that context in Claude. 292 > 293 > Use the full installer only when you are doing a fully manual ECC install instead of the plugin path. 294 > 295 > If your local Claude setup was wiped or reset, that does not mean you need to repurchase ECC. Start with `node scripts/ecc.js list-installed`, then run `node scripts/ecc.js doctor` and `node scripts/ecc.js repair` before reinstalling anything. That usually restores ECC-managed files without rebuilding your setup. If the problem is account or marketplace access for ECC Tools, handle billing/account recovery separately. 296 297 ```bash 298 # Clone the repo first 299 git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code.git 300 cd everything-claude-code 301 302 # Install dependencies (pick your package manager) 303 npm install # or: pnpm install | yarn install | bun install 304 305 # Plugin install path: copy only ECC rules into an ECC-owned namespace 306 mkdir -p ~/.claude/rules/ecc 307 cp -R rules/common ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 308 cp -R rules/typescript ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 309 310 # Fully manual ECC install path (use this instead of /plugin install) 311 # ./install.sh --profile full 312 ``` 313 314 ```powershell 315 # Windows PowerShell 316 317 # Plugin install path: copy only ECC rules into an ECC-owned namespace 318 New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "$HOME/.claude/rules/ecc" | Out-Null 319 Copy-Item -Recurse rules/common "$HOME/.claude/rules/ecc/" 320 Copy-Item -Recurse rules/typescript "$HOME/.claude/rules/ecc/" 321 322 # Fully manual ECC install path (use this instead of /plugin install) 323 # .\install.ps1 --profile full 324 # npx ecc-install --profile full 325 ``` 326 327 For manual install instructions see the README in the `rules/` folder. When copying rules manually, copy the whole language directory (for example `rules/common` or `rules/golang`), not the files inside it, so relative references keep working and filenames do not collide. 328 329 ### Fully manual install (Fallback) 330 331 Use this only if you are intentionally skipping the plugin path: 332 333 ```bash 334 ./install.sh --profile full 335 ``` 336 337 ```powershell 338 .\install.ps1 --profile full 339 # or 340 npx ecc-install --profile full 341 ``` 342 343 If you choose this path, stop there. Do not also run `/plugin install`. 344 345 ### Reset / Uninstall ECC 346 347 If ECC feels duplicated, intrusive, or broken, do not keep reinstalling it on top of itself. 348 349 - **Plugin path:** remove the plugin from Claude Code, then delete the specific rule folders you manually copied under `~/.claude/rules/ecc/`. 350 - **Manual installer / CLI path:** from the repo root, preview removal first: 351 352 ```bash 353 node scripts/uninstall.js --dry-run 354 ``` 355 356 Then remove ECC-managed files: 357 358 ```bash 359 node scripts/uninstall.js 360 ``` 361 362 You can also use the lifecycle wrapper: 363 364 ```bash 365 node scripts/ecc.js list-installed 366 node scripts/ecc.js doctor 367 node scripts/ecc.js repair 368 node scripts/ecc.js uninstall --dry-run 369 ``` 370 371 ECC only removes files recorded in its install-state. It will not delete unrelated files it did not install. 372 373 If you stacked methods, clean up in this order: 374 375 1. Remove the Claude Code plugin install. 376 2. Run the ECC uninstall command from the repo root to remove install-state-managed files. 377 3. Delete any extra rule folders you copied manually and no longer want. 378 4. Reinstall once, using a single path. 379 380 ### Step 3: Start Using 381 382 ```bash 383 # Skills are the primary workflow surface. 384 # Existing slash-style command names still work while ECC migrates off commands/. 385 386 # Plugin install uses the canonical namespaced form 387 /ecc:plan "Add user authentication" 388 389 # Manual install keeps the shorter slash form: 390 # /plan "Add user authentication" 391 392 # Check available commands 393 /plugin list ecc@ecc 394 ``` 395 396 **That's it!** You now have access to 60 agents, 229 skills, and 75 legacy command shims. 397 398 ### Dashboard GUI 399 400 Launch the desktop dashboard to visually explore ECC components: 401 402 ```bash 403 npm run dashboard 404 # or 405 python3 ./ecc_dashboard.py 406 ``` 407 408 **Features:** 409 - Tabbed interface: Agents, Skills, Commands, Rules, Settings 410 - Dark/Light theme toggle 411 - Font customization (family & size) 412 - Project logo in header and taskbar 413 - Search and filter across all components 414 415 ### Multi-model commands require additional setup 416 417 > WARNING: `multi-*` commands are **not** covered by the base plugin/rules install above. 418 > 419 > To use `/multi-plan`, `/multi-execute`, `/multi-backend`, `/multi-frontend`, and `/multi-workflow`, you must also install the `ccg-workflow` runtime. 420 > 421 > Initialize it with `npx ccg-workflow`. 422 > 423 > That runtime provides the external dependencies these commands expect, including: 424 > - `~/.claude/bin/codeagent-wrapper` 425 > - `~/.claude/.ccg/prompts/*` 426 > 427 > Without `ccg-workflow`, these `multi-*` commands will not run correctly. 428 429 --- 430 431 ## Cross-Platform Support 432 433 This plugin now fully supports **Windows, macOS, and Linux**, alongside tight integration across major IDEs (Cursor, OpenCode, Antigravity) and CLI harnesses. All hooks and scripts have been rewritten in Node.js for maximum compatibility. 434 435 ### Package Manager Detection 436 437 The plugin automatically detects your preferred package manager (npm, pnpm, yarn, or bun) with the following priority: 438 439 1. **Environment variable**: `CLAUDE_PACKAGE_MANAGER` 440 2. **Project config**: `.claude/package-manager.json` 441 3. **package.json**: `packageManager` field 442 4. **Lock file**: Detection from package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, or bun.lockb 443 5. **Global config**: `~/.claude/package-manager.json` 444 6. **Fallback**: First available package manager 445 446 To set your preferred package manager: 447 448 ```bash 449 # Via environment variable 450 export CLAUDE_PACKAGE_MANAGER=pnpm 451 452 # Via global config 453 node scripts/setup-package-manager.js --global pnpm 454 455 # Via project config 456 node scripts/setup-package-manager.js --project bun 457 458 # Detect current setting 459 node scripts/setup-package-manager.js --detect 460 ``` 461 462 Or use the `/setup-pm` command in Claude Code. 463 464 ### Hook Runtime Controls 465 466 Use runtime flags to tune strictness or disable specific hooks temporarily: 467 468 ```bash 469 # Hook strictness profile (default: standard) 470 export ECC_HOOK_PROFILE=standard 471 472 # Comma-separated hook IDs to disable 473 export ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS="pre:bash:tmux-reminder,post:edit:typecheck" 474 475 # Cap SessionStart additional context (default: 8000 chars) 476 export ECC_SESSION_START_MAX_CHARS=4000 477 478 # Disable SessionStart additional context entirely for low-context/local-model setups 479 export ECC_SESSION_START_CONTEXT=off 480 ``` 481 482 --- 483 484 ## What's Inside 485 486 This repo is a **Claude Code plugin** - install it directly or copy components manually. 487 488 ``` 489 everything-claude-code/ 490 |-- .claude-plugin/ # Analyze current repo 715 /skill-create --instincts # Also generate instincts for continuous-learning-v2 716 ``` 717 718 This analyzes your git history locally and generates SKILL.md files. 719 720 #### Option B: GitHub App (Advanced) 721 722 For advanced features (10k+ commits, auto-PRs, team sharing): 723 724 [Install GitHub App](https://github.com/apps/skill-creator) | [ecc.tools](https://ecc.tools) 725 726 ```bash 727 # Comment on any issue: 728 /skill-creator analyze 729 730 # Or auto-triggers on push to default branch 731 ``` 732 733 Both options create: 734 - **SKILL.md files** - Ready-to-use skills for Claude Code 735 - **Instinct collections** - For continuous-learning-v2 736 - **Pattern extraction** - Learns from your commit history 737 738 ### AgentShield — Security Auditor 739 740 > Built at the Claude Code Hackathon (Cerebral Valley x Anthropic, Feb 2026). 1282 tests, 98% coverage, 102 static analysis rules. 741 742 Scan your Claude Code configuration for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and injection risks. 743 744 ```bash 745 # Quick scan (no install needed) 746 npx ecc-agentshield scan 747 748 # Auto-fix safe issues 749 npx ecc-agentshield scan --fix 750 751 # Deep analysis with three Opus 4.6 agents 752 npx ecc-agentshield scan --opus --stream 753 754 # Generate secure config from scratch 755 npx ecc-agentshield init 756 ``` 757 758 **What it scans:** CLAUDE.md, settings.json, MCP configs, hooks, agent definitions, and skills across 5 categories — secrets detection (14 patterns), permission auditing, hook injection analysis, MCP server risk profiling, and agent config review. 759 760 **The `--opus` flag** runs three Claude Opus 4.6 agents in a red-team/blue-team/auditor pipeline. The attacker finds exploit chains, the defender evaluates protections, and the auditor synthesizes both into a prioritized risk assessment. Adversarial reasoning, not just pattern matching. 761 762 **Output formats:** Terminal (color-graded A-F), JSON (CI pipelines), Markdown, HTML. Exit code 2 on critical findings for build gates. 763 764 Use `/security-scan` in Claude Code to run it, or add to CI with the [GitHub Action](https://github.com/affaan-m/agentshield). 765 766 [GitHub](https://github.com/affaan-m/agentshield) | [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/ecc-agentshield) 767 768 ### Continuous Learning v2 769 770 The instinct-based learning system automatically learns your patterns: 771 772 ```bash 773 /instinct-status # Show learned instincts with confidence 774 /instinct-import # Import instincts from others 775 /instinct-export # Export your instincts for sharing 776 /evolve # Cluster related instincts into skills 777 ``` 778 779 See `skills/continuous-learning-v2/` for full documentation. 780 Keep `continuous-learning/` only when you explicitly want the legacy v1 Stop-hook learned-skill flow. 781 782 --- 783 784 ## Requirements 785 786 ### Claude Code CLI Version 787 788 **Minimum version: v2.1.0 or later** 789 790 This plugin requires Claude Code CLI v2.1.0+ due to changes in how the plugin system handles hooks. 791 792 Check your version: 793 ```bash 794 claude --version 795 ``` 796 797 ### Important: Hooks Auto-Loading Behavior 798 799 > WARNING: **For Contributors:** Do NOT add a `"hooks"` field to `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`. This is enforced by a regression test. 800 801 Claude Code v2.1+ **automatically loads** `hooks/hooks.json` from any installed plugin by convention. Explicitly declaring it in `plugin.json` causes a duplicate detection error: 802 803 ``` 804 Duplicate hooks file detected: ./hooks/hooks.json resolves to already-loaded file 805 ``` 806 807 **History:** This has caused repeated fix/revert cycles in this repo ([#29](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/issues/29), [#52](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/issues/52), [#103](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/issues/103)). The behavior changed between Claude Code versions, leading to confusion. We now have a regression test to prevent this from being reintroduced. 808 809 --- 810 811 ## Installation 812 813 ### Option 1: Install as Plugin (Recommended) 814 815 The easiest way to use this repo - install as a Claude Code plugin: 816 817 ```bash 818 # Add this repo as a marketplace 819 /plugin marketplace add https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code 820 821 # Install the plugin 822 /plugin install ecc@ecc 823 ``` 824 825 Or add directly to your `~/.claude/settings.json`: 826 827 ```json 828 { 829 "extraKnownMarketplaces": { 830 "ecc": { 831 "source": { 832 "source": "github", 833 "repo": "affaan-m/everything-claude-code" 834 } 835 } 836 }, 837 "enabledPlugins": { 838 "ecc@ecc": true 839 } 840 } 841 ``` 842 843 This gives you instant access to all commands, agents, skills, and hooks. 844 845 > **Note:** The Claude Code plugin system does not support distributing `rules` via plugins ([upstream limitation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference)). You need to install rules manually: 846 > 847 > ```bash 848 > # Clone the repo first 849 > git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code.git 850 > 851 > # Option A: User-level rules (applies to all projects) 852 > mkdir -p ~/.claude/rules/ecc 853 > cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/common ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 854 > cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/typescript ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ # pick your stack 855 > cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/python ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 856 > cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/golang ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 857 > cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/php ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 858 > 859 > # Option B: Project-level rules (applies to current project only) 860 > mkdir -p .claude/rules/ecc 861 > cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/common .claude/rules/ecc/ 862 > cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/typescript .claude/rules/ecc/ # pick your stack 863 > ``` 864 865 --- 866 867 ### Option 2: Manual Installation 868 869 If you prefer manual control over what's installed: 870 871 ```bash 872 # Clone the repo 873 git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code.git 874 875 # Copy agents to your Claude config 876 cp everything-claude-code/agents/*.md ~/.claude/agents/ 877 878 # Copy rules directories (common + language-specific) 879 mkdir -p ~/.claude/rules/ecc 880 cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/common ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 881 cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/typescript ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ # pick your stack 882 cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/python ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 883 cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/golang ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 884 cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/php ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 885 cp -r everything-claude-code/rules/arkts ~/.claude/rules/ecc/ 886 887 # Copy skills first (primary workflow surface) 888 # Recommended (new users): core/general skills only 889 mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/ecc 890 cp -r everything-claude-code/.agents/skills/* ~/.claude/skills/ecc/ 891 cp -r everything-claude-code/skills/search-first ~/.claude/skills/ecc/ 892 893 # Optional: add niche/framework-specific skills only when needed 894 # for s in django-patterns django-tdd laravel-patterns springboot-patterns quarkus-patterns; do 895 # cp -r everything-claude-code/skills/$s ~/.claude/skills/ecc/ 896 # done 897 898 # Optional: keep maintained slash-command compatibility during migration 899 mkdir -p ~/.claude/commands 900 cp everything-claude-code/commands/*.md ~/.claude/commands/ 901 902 # Retired shims live in legacy-command-shims/commands/. 903 # Copy individual files from there only if you still need old names such as /tdd. 904 ``` 905 906 #### Install hooks 907 908 Do not copy the raw repo `hooks/hooks.json` into `~/.claude/settings.json` or `~/.claude/hooks/hooks.json`. That file is plugin/repo-oriented and is meant to be installed through the ECC installer or loaded as a plugin, so raw copying is not a supported manual install path. 909 910 Use the installer to install only the Claude hook runtime so command paths are rewritten correctly: 911 912 ```bash 913 # macOS / Linux 914 bash ./install.sh --target claude --modules hooks-runtime 915 ``` 916 917 ```powershell 918 # Windows PowerShell 919 pwsh -File .\install.ps1 --target claude --modules hooks-runtime 920 ``` 921 922 That writes resolved hooks to `~/.claude/hooks/hooks.json` and leaves any existing `~/.claude/settings.json` untouched. 923 924 If you installed ECC via `/plugin install`, do not copy those hooks into `settings.json`. Claude Code v2.1+ already auto-loads plugin `hooks/hooks.json`, and duplicating them in `settings.json` causes duplicate execution and cross-platform hook conflicts. 925 926 Windows note: the Claude config directory is `%USERPROFILE%\\.claude`, not `~/claude`. 927 928 #### Configure MCPs 929 930 Claude plugin installs intentionally do not auto-enable ECC's bundled MCP server definitions. This avoids overlong plugin MCP tool names on strict third-party gateways while keeping manual MCP setup available. 931 932 Use Claude Code's `/mcp` command or CLI-managed MCP setup for live Claude Code server changes. Use `/mcp` for Claude Code runtime disables; Claude Code persists those choices in `~/.claude.json`. 933 934 For repo-local MCP access, copy desired MCP server definitions from `mcp-configs/mcp-servers.json` into a project-scoped `.mcp.json`. 935 936 If you already run your own copies of ECC-bundled MCPs, set: 937 938 ```bash 939 export ECC_DISABLED_MCPS="github,context7,exa,playwright,sequential-thinking,memory" 940 ``` 941 942 ECC-managed install and Codex sync flows will skip or remove those bundled servers instead of re-adding duplicates. `ECC_DISABLED_MCPS` is an ECC install/sync filter, not a live Claude Code toggle. 943 944 **Important:** Replace `YOUR_*_HERE` placeholders with your actual API keys. 945 946 --- 947 948 ## Key Concepts 949 950 ### Agents 951 952 Subagents handle delegated tasks with limited scope. Example: 953 954 ```markdown 955 --- 956 name: code-reviewer 957 description: Reviews code for quality, security, and maintainability 958 tools: ["Read", "Grep", "Glob", "Bash"] 959 model: opus 960 --- 961 962 You are a senior code reviewer... 963 ``` 964 965 ### Skills 966 967 Skills are the primary workflow surface. They can be invoked directly, suggested automatically, and reused by agents. ECC still ships maintained `commands/` during migration, while retired short-name shims live under `legacy-command-shims/` for explicit opt-in only. New workflow development should land in `skills/` first. 968 969 ```markdown 970 # TDD Workflow 971 972 1. Define interfaces first 973 2. Write failing tests (RED) 974 3. Implement minimal code (GREEN) 975 4. Refactor (IMPROVE) 976 5. Verify 80%+ coverage 977 ``` 978 979 ### Hooks 980 981 Hooks fire on tool events. Example - warn about console.log: 982 983 ```json 984 { 985 "matcher": "tool == \"Edit\" && tool_input.file_path matches \"\\\\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$\"", 986 "hooks": [{ 987 "type": "command", 988 "command": "#!/bin/bash\ngrep -n 'console\\.log' \"$file_path\" && echo '[Hook] Remove console.log' >&2" 989 }] 990 } 991 ``` 992 993 ### Rules 994 995 Rules are always-follow guidelines, organized into `common/` (language-agnostic) + language-specific directories: 996 997 ``` 998 rules/ 999 common/ # Universal principles (always install) 1000 typescript/ # TS/JS specific patterns and tools 1001 python/ # Python specific patterns and tools 1002 golang/ # Go specific patterns and tools 1003 swift/ # Swift specific patterns and tools 1004 php/ # PHP specific patterns and tools 1005 arkts/ # HarmonyOS / ArkTS patterns and constraints 1006 ``` 1007 1008 See [`rules/README.md`](rules/README.md) for installation and structure details. 1009 1010 --- 1011 1012 ## Which Agent Should I Use? 1013 1014 Not sure where to start? Use this quick reference. Skills are the canonical workflow surface; maintained slash entries stay available for command-first workflows. 1015 1016 | I want to... | Use this surface | Agent used | 1017 |--------------|-----------------|------------| 1018 | Plan a new feature | `/ecc:plan "Add auth"` | planner | 1019 | Design system architecture | `/ecc:plan` + architect agent | architect | 1020 | Write code with tests first | `tdd-workflow` skill | tdd-guide | 1021 | Review code I just wrote | `/code-review` | code-reviewer | 1022 | Fix a failing build | `/build-fix` | build-error-resolver | 1023 | Run end-to-end tests | `e2e-testing` skill | e2e-runner | 1024 | Find security vulnerabilities | `/security-scan` | security-reviewer | 1025 | Remove dead code | `/refactor-clean` | refactor-cleaner | 1026 | Update documentation | `/update-docs` | doc-updater | 1027 | Review Go code | `/go-review` | go-reviewer | 1028 | Review Python code | `/python-review` | python-reviewer | 1029 | Review F# code | *(invoke `fsharp-reviewer` directly)* | fsharp-reviewer | 1030 | Review TypeScript/JavaScript code | *(invoke `typescript-reviewer` directly)* | typescript-reviewer | 1031 | Develop HarmonyOS apps | *(invoke `harmonyos-app-resolver` directly)* | harmonyos-app-resolver | 1032 | Audit database queries | *(auto-delegated)* | database-reviewer | 1033 | Review production ML changes | `mle-workflow` skill + `mle-reviewer` agent | mle-reviewer | 1034 1035 ### Common Workflows 1036 1037 Slash forms below are shown where they remain part of the maintained command surface. Retired short-name shims such as `/tdd` and `/eval` live in `legacy-command-shims/` for explicit opt-in only. 1038 1039 **Starting a new feature:** 1040 ``` 1041 /ecc:plan "Add user authentication with OAuth" 1042 → planner creates implementation blueprint 1043 tdd-workflow skill → tdd-guide enforces write-tests-first 1044 /code-review → code-reviewer checks your work 1045 ``` 1046 1047 **Fixing a bug:** 1048 ``` 1049 tdd-workflow skill → tdd-guide: write a failing test that reproduces it 1050 → implement the fix, verify test passes 1051 /code-review → code-reviewer: catch regressions 1052 ``` 1053 1054 **Preparing for production:** 1055 ``` 1056 /security-scan → security-reviewer: OWASP Top 10 audit 1057 e2e-testing skill → e2e-runner: critical user flow tests 1058 /test-coverage → verify 80%+ coverage 1059 ``` 1060 1061 --- 1062 1063 ## FAQ 1064 1065
1066 How do I check which agents/commands are installed? 1067 1068 ```bash 1069 /plugin list ecc@ecc 1070 ``` 1071 1072 This shows all available agents, commands, and skills from the plugin. 1073

1076 My hooks aren't working / I see "Duplicate hooks file" errors 1077 1078 This is the most common issue. **Do NOT add a `"hooks"` field to `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`.** Claude Code v2.1+ automatically loads `hooks/hooks.json` from installed plugins. Explicitly declaring it causes duplicate detection errors. See [#29](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/issues/29), [#52](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/issues/52), [#103](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/issues/103). 1079

1082 Can I use ECC with Claude Code on a custom API endpoint or model gateway? 1083 1084 Yes. ECC does not hardcode Anthropic-hosted transport settings. It runs locally through Claude Code's normal CLI/plugin surface, so it works with: 1085 1086 - Anthropic-hosted Claude Code 1087 - Official Claude Code gateway setups using `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` and `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` 1088 - Compatible custom endpoints that speak the Anthropic API Claude Code expects 1089 1090 Minimal example: 1091 1092 ```bash 1093 export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://your-gateway.example.com 1094 export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=your-token 1095 claude 1096 ``` 1097 1098 If your gateway remaps model names, configure that in Claude Code rather than in ECC. ECC's hooks, skills, commands, and rules are model-provider agnostic once the `claude` CLI is already working. 1099 1100 Official references: 1101 - [Claude Code LLM gateway docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/llm-gateway) 1102 - [Claude Code model configuration docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/model-config) 1103 1104
1105 1106
1107 My context window is shrinking / Claude is running out of context 1108 1109 Too many MCP servers eat your context. Each MCP tool description consumes tokens from your 200k window, potentially reducing it to ~70k. SessionStart context is capped at 8000 characters by default; lower it with `ECC_SESSION_START_MAX_CHARS=4000` or disable it with `ECC_SESSION_START_CONTEXT=off` for local-model or low-context setups. 1110 1111 **Fix:** Disable unused MCPs from Claude Code with `/mcp`. Claude Code writes those runtime choices to `~/.claude.json`; `.claude/settings.json` and `.claude/settings.local.json` are not reliable toggles for already-loaded MCP servers. 1112 1113 Keep under 10 MCPs enabled and under 80 tools active. 1114

1134 Does this work with Cursor / OpenCode / Codex / Antigravity / GitHub Copilot? 1135 1136 Yes. ECC is cross-platform: 1137 - **Cursor**: Pre-translated configs in `.cursor/`. See [Cursor IDE Support](#cursor-ide-support). 1138 - **Gemini CLI**: Experimental project-local support via `.gemini/GEMINI.md` and shared installer plumbing. 1139 - **OpenCode**: Full plugin support in `.opencode/`. See [OpenCode Support](#opencode-support). 1140 - **Codex**: First-class support for both macOS app and CLI, with adapter drift guards and SessionStart fallback. See PR [#257](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/pull/257). 1141 - **GitHub Copilot (VS Code)**: Instruction and prompt layer via `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `.vscode/settings.json`, and `.github/prompts/`. See [GitHub Copilot Support](#github-copilot-support). 1142 - **Antigravity**: Tightly integrated setup for workflows, skills, and flattened rules in `.agent/`. See [Antigravity Guide](docs/ANTIGRAVITY-GUIDE.md). 1143 - **JoyCode / CodeBuddy**: Project-local selective install adapters for commands, agents, skills, and flattened rules. See [JoyCode Adapter Guide](docs/JOYCODE-GUIDE.md). 1144 - **Qwen CLI**: Home-directory selective install adapter for commands, agents, skills, rules, and Qwen config. See [Qwen CLI Adapter Guide](docs/QWEN-GUIDE.md). 1145 - **Non-native harnesses**: Manual fallback path for Grok and similar interfaces. See [Manual Adaptation Guide](docs/MANUAL-ADAPTATION-GUIDE.md). 1146 - **Claude Code**: Native — this is the primary target. 1147

[claude-seo](https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo) — SEO-focused skill and agent collection 1202 - [claude-ads](https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-ads) — Ad-audit and paid-growth workflow collection 1203 - [claude-cybersecurity](https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-cybersecurity) — Security-oriented skill and agent collection 1204 1205 --- 1206 1207 ## Cursor IDE Support 1208 1209 ECC provides Cursor IDE support with hooks, rules, agents, skills, commands, and MCP configs adapted for Cursor's project layout. 1210 1211 ### Quick Start (Cursor) 1212 1213 ```bash 1214 # macOS/Linux 1215 ./install.sh --target cursor typescript 1216 ./install.sh --target cursor python golang swift php 1217 ``` 1218 1219 ```powershell 1220 # Windows PowerShell 1221 .\install.ps1 --target cursor typescript 1222 .\install.ps1 --target cursor python golang swift php 1223 ``` 1224 1225 ### What's Included 1226 1227 | Component | Count | Details | 1228 |-----------|-------|---------| 1229 | Hook Events | 15 | sessionStart, beforeShellExecution, afterFileEdit, beforeMCPExecution, beforeSubmitPrompt, and 10 more | 1230 | Hook Scripts | 16 | Thin Node.js scripts delegating to `scripts/hooks/` via shared adapter | 1231 | Rules | 34 | 9 common (alwaysApply) + 25 language-specific (TypeScript, Python, Go, Swift, PHP) | 1232 | Agents | 48 | `.cursor/agents/ecc-*.md` when installed; prefixed to avoid collisions with user or marketplace agents | 1233 | Skills | Shared + Bundled | `.cursor/skills/` for translated additions | 1234 | Commands | Shared | `.cursor/commands/` if installed | 1235 | MCP Config | Shared | `.cursor/mcp.json` if installed | 1236 1237 ### Cursor Loading Notes 1238 1239 ECC does not install root `AGENTS.md` into `.cursor/`. Cursor treats nested `AGENTS.md` files as directory context, so copying ECC's repo identity into a host project would pollute that project. 1240 1241 Cursor-native loading behavior can vary by Cursor build. ECC installs agents as `.cursor/agents/ecc-*.md`; if your Cursor build does not expose project agents, those files still work as explicit reference definitions instead of hidden global prompt context. 1242 1243 ### Hook Architecture (DRY Adapter Pattern) 1244 1245 Cursor has **more hook events than Claude Code** (20 vs 8). The `.cursor/hooks/adapter.js` module transforms Cursor's stdin JSON to Claude Code's format, allowing existing `scripts/hooks/*.js` to be reused without duplication. 1246 1247 ``` 1248 Cursor stdin JSON → adapter.js → transforms → scripts/hooks/*.js 1249 (shared with Claude Code) 1250 ``` 1251 1252 Key hooks: 1253 - **beforeShellExecution** — Blocks dev servers outside tmux (exit 2), git push review 1254 - **afterFileEdit** — Auto-format + TypeScript check + console.log warning 1255 - **beforeSubmitPrompt** — Detects secrets (sk-, ghp_, AKIA patterns) in prompts 1256 - **beforeTabFileRead** — Blocks Tab from reading .env, .key, .pem files (exit 2) 1257 - **beforeMCPExecution / afterMCPExecution** — MCP audit logging 1258 1259 ### Rules Format 1260 1261 Cursor rules use YAML frontmatter with `description`, `globs`, and `alwaysApply`: 1262 1263 ```yaml 1264 --- 1265 description: "TypeScript coding style extending common rules" 1266 globs: ["**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", "**/*.js", "**/*.jsx"] 1267 alwaysApply: false 1268 --- 1269 ``` 1270 1271 --- 1272 1273 ## Codex macOS App + CLI Support 1274 1275 ECC provides **first-class Codex support** for both the macOS app and CLI, with a reference configuration, Codex-specific AGENTS.md supplement, and shared skills. 1276 1277 ### Quick Start (Codex App + CLI) 1278 1279 ```bash 1280 # Run Codex CLI in the repo — AGENTS.md and .codex/ are auto-detected 1281 codex 1282 1283 # Automatic setup: sync ECC assets (AGENTS.md, skills, MCP servers) into ~/.codex 1284 npm install && bash scripts/sync-ecc-to-codex.sh 1285 # or: pnpm install && bash scripts/sync-ecc-to-codex.sh 1286 # or: yarn install && bash scripts/sync-ecc-to-codex.sh 1287 # or: bun install && bash scripts/sync-ecc-to-codex.sh 1288 1289 # Or manually: copy the reference config to your home directory 1290 cp .codex/config.toml ~/.codex/config.toml 1291 ``` 1292 1293 The sync script safely merges ECC MCP servers into your existing `~/.codex/config.toml` using an **add-only** strategy — it never removes or modifies your existing servers. Run with `--dry-run` to preview changes, or `--update-mcp` to force-refresh ECC servers to the latest recommended config. 1294 1295 For Context7, ECC uses the canonical Codex section name `[mcp_servers.context7]` while still launching the `@upstash/context7-mcp` package. If you already have a legacy `[mcp_servers.context7-mcp]` entry, `--update-mcp` migrates it to the canonical section name. 1296 1297 Codex macOS app: 1298 - Open this repository as your workspace. 1299 - The root `AGENTS.md` is auto-detected. 1300 - `.codex/config.toml` and `.codex/agents/*.toml` work best when kept project-local. 1301 - The reference `.codex/config.toml` intentionally does not pin `model` or `model_provider`, so Codex uses its own current default unless you override it. 1302 - Optional: copy `.codex/config.toml` to `~/.codex/config.toml` for global defaults; keep the multi-agent role files project-local unless you also copy `.codex/agents/`. 1303 1304 ### What's Included 1305 1306 | Component | Count | Details | 1307 |-----------|-------|---------| 1308 | Config | 1 | `.codex/config.toml` — top-level approvals/sandbox/web_search, MCP servers, notifications, profiles | 1309 | AGENTS.md | 2 | Root (universal) + `.codex/AGENTS.md` (Codex-specific supplement) | 1310 | Skills | 32 | `.agents/skills/` — SKILL.md + agents/openai.yaml per skill | 1311 | MCP Servers | 6 | GitHub, Context7, Exa, Memory, Playwright, Sequential Thinking (7 with Supabase via `--update-mcp` sync) | 1312 | Profiles | 2 | `strict` (read-only sandbox) and `yolo` (full auto-approve) | 1313 | Agent Roles | 3 | `.codex/agents/` — explorer, reviewer, docs-researcher | 1314 1315 ### Skills 1316 1317 Skills at `.agents/skills/` are auto-loaded by Codex: 1318 1319 Canonical Anthropic skills such as `claude-api`, `frontend-design`, and `skill-creator` are intentionally not re-bundled here.
